source_jobs.title: Enterprise Transformation Manager
source_jobs.description:
Hi there! Thanks for stopping by 👋

Are you actively looking for a new opportunity? Or just checking the market? Well… you might just be in the right place!

We’re looking for an Enterprise Transformation Manager to join our team at Lightspeed. This role will serve as a key member of Lightspeed’s Transformation Management Office (TMO) and report to Lightspeed’s Director of Enterprise Transformation, driving the weekly drumbeat of execution across multiple high-priority transformation workstreams. Lightspeed is currently undergoing a multi-year transformation focused on strengthening core growth engines and driving operational efficiency across the business. In this role, you will help bring strategy to life by translating priorities into action, removing roadblocks, and ensuring initiatives deliver measurable impact. The ideal candidate combines strong strategic thinking with a bias toward execution and experience navigating complex, cross-functional environments.

What you'll be doing:

 - Drive high-priority transformation initiatives from concept through execution, ensuring accountability and delivery of measurable outcomes

 - Translate strategic priorities into actionable plans, bringing strategy to life through clear execution plans, ownership, and outcomes

 - Act as the drumbeat behind execution across transformation workstreams, maintaining momentum and ensuring consistent progress week over week

 - Partner closely with the broader transformation team, workstream leads and initiative owners, and other cross-functional teams to align priorities, drive accountability, and unblock critical issues

 - Proactively identify risks, dependencies, and roadblocks, and take action to resolve them and keep initiatives on track

 - Track progress against key milestones and value targets, ensuring initiatives deliver measurable financial and operational impact

What you need to bring:

 - Strong structured problem-solving skills, with the ability to connect strategy to execution and drive outcomes

 - Demonstrated experience leading complex, cross-functional initiatives in fast-paced, matrixed environments

 - Proven ability to operate with a bias toward action and act as a driver of execution, not just a coordinator

 - Strong stakeholder management skills, with the ability to influence senior executives and drive cross-functional alignment without direct authority

 - Excellent communication skills, including experience synthesizing insights and supporting executive-level decision-making

 - Strong business acumen, with the ability to connect initiative progress to broader strategic priorities

 - As a global company with employees and clients outside of Quebec, fluency in English as a working language is required for this position.

Even better if you have, but not necessary:

 - 2-4+ years of experience in consulting, strategy, business operations, or a related field

 - Experience working on or within large-scale transformation programs or enterprise-wide initiatives

 - Experience in SaaS, fintech, or other high-growth, technology-driven environments

You’ll enjoy:

 - A flexible work environment that empowers you to do your best work

 - A culture that celebrates performance

 - The chance to make an impact in a team that’s big enough for career growth, but lean enough to make your voice heard

 - Career-defining opportunities

Plus benefits designed to keep you happy, healthy and fulfilled.

 - Flexible paid time off and remote work policies

 - Equity options, because this is your company too

 - Contributions to your pension plan. Your future matters

 - Training opportunities to grow your skills and career

 - Health and wellness credit so you feel your best

 - Time off to volunteer and give back to your community

 - Interest groups, employee led networks, social committees to sponsored sports teams

 - Computer purchase program to get your personal MacBook

 - Enhanced parental leave to support growing families

source_jobs.title: Head of Revenue Operations
source_jobs.description:
Hi there! Thanks for stopping by 👋

Are you actively looking for a new opportunity? Or just checking the market? Well… you might just be in the right place!

We’re looking for a Head of Revenue Operations to join our team. Reporting directly to the SVP Systems & Revenue Operations, this position will oversee the global functions of Revenue Operations, Planning, Forecasting, Sales commissions and regional sales operations and Go-To-Market tools and systems.

This role will collaborate directly with Sales, Marketing, and Finance teams to determine change to GTM strategy & planning, implement performance metrics and influence alignment of all revenue operations.

What you'll be doing:

 - Drive effective revenue opåerational management within the Go-To-Market function

 - Manage revenue growth strategy and execution, operations, enablement and data analysis to support global Go-To-Market initiatives

 - Ensure global alignment across sales functions

 - Collaborate with leadership to drive annual revenue operations planning cycles and alignment across cross-functional businesses

 - Develop and influence new processes and technologies that optimize the path for supporting the revenue growth of the businesses globally across all routes to market

 - Develop and nurture cross-functional partnerships across the entire organization which impact overall growth

 - Partner with leadership to identify strategic priorities and opportunities for the team, overseeing objectives and key results, and ensuring focus is on highest-impact initiatives

 - Partner with relevant GTM Teams and Sales Strategy to leverage performance analytics, influence cross-functional revenue programs and escalate relevant topics

 - Build strong working relationships with executives and key leaders across GTM teams (e.g. product, engineering, marketing, information technology and finance) and influence the goals and priorities of stakeholders whose mission aligns with that of Revenue Operations

 - Build, develop and manage a diverse team of high-performing managers: Build and maintain a world-class, inclusive, diverse, high-performing team by driving effective onboarding, on-going training, retention with a strong remote culture

What you need to bring:

 - 8+ years experience in Strategy, FP&A, GTM Operations and in a People Management role

 - Experience in business, operational leadership roles in a global SaaS Sales environment

 - Experience in crafting strategic vision, making data-driven business decisions, including identifying gaps or business needs, and innovating and scaling solutions

 - Extensive experience in a high-impact strategic role in a fast-growth global organisation

 - MBA degree preferred

 - AI transformation experience preferred within Rev Ops context

 - Management experience and skills with proven experience influencing in a highly-matrixed company

 - Proven record of leading a diverse, high performing team across geographical regions and experience building high-performing, inclusive teams

 - Strong ability to interact and influence effectively with all C-level executives and senior leaders

 - As a global company with employees and clients outside of Quebec, fluency in English as a working language is required for this position.

 - Ability to understand key performance indicators, both quantitative and qualitative in a compelling way

 - Strong analytical background; prior experience in a strategic marketing role and/or strategy consulting would be helpful

We know that people are more than what’s on their CV. If you’re unsure that you have the right profile for the role... hit the ‘Apply’ button and give it a try!

Be a changemaker

You’ll enjoy:

 - A flexible work environment that empowers you to do your best work

 - A culture that celebrates performance

 - The chance to make an impact in a team that’s big enough for career growth, but lean enough to make your voice heard

 - Career-defining opportunities

source_jobs.title: Directeur(trice) principal(e), Opérations des revenus
source_jobs.description:
Bonjour! Merci de votre visite 👋

Êtes-vous activement à la recherche d’une nouvelle opportunité ou simplement en train d’explorer le marché? Dans tous les cas… vous êtes peut-être au bon endroit!

Nous sommes à la recherche d’un(e) Directeur(trice) principal(e), Opérations des revenus pour se joindre à notre équipe. Relevant directement du SVP, Systèmes et Opérations des revenus, cette personne supervisera à l’échelle mondiale les fonctions liées aux opérations des revenus, à la planification, aux prévisions, aux commissions de vente, aux opérations commerciales régionales ainsi qu’aux outils et systèmes Go-To-Market (GTM).

Dans ce rôle, vous collaborerez étroitement avec les équipes des ventes, du marketing et des finances afin d’orienter la stratégie GTM, de mettre en place des indicateurs de performance et de favoriser l’alignement de l’ensemble des fonctions liées aux revenus.

Ce que vous ferez:

 - Assurer une gestion efficace des opérations des revenus au sein de la fonction Go-To-Market.

 - Diriger la stratégie et l’exécution de la croissance des revenus, les opérations, l’habilitation ainsi que l’analyse de données afin de soutenir les initiatives GTM à l’échelle mondiale.

 - Veiller à l’alignement global entre les différentes fonctions de vente.

 - Collaborer avec les équipes de direction pour piloter les cycles annuels de planification des opérations des revenus et assurer l’alignement entre les différentes unités d’affaires.

 - Développer et influencer la mise en place de nouveaux processus et technologies permettant d’optimiser la croissance des revenus à travers tous les canaux de mise en marché.

 - Développer et entretenir des partenariats interfonctionnels à travers l’organisation afin de soutenir la croissance globale de l’entreprise.

 - Travailler avec les équipes de direction afin d’identifier les priorités stratégiques et les opportunités pour l’équipe, superviser les objectifs et résultats clés (OKRs) et assurer un focus sur les initiatives ayant le plus grand impact.

 - Collaborer avec les équipes GTM et l’équipe de stratégie des ventes afin d’exploiter les analyses de performance, influencer les programmes interfonctionnels liés aux revenus et faire remonter les enjeux pertinents.

 - Établir de solides relations de travail avec les dirigeants et les principaux intervenants des équipes GTM (produit, ingénierie, marketing, technologies de l’information, finances, etc.) et influencer leurs priorités afin d’assurer l’atteinte des objectifs des Opérations des revenus.

 - Recruter, développer et gérer une équipe diversifiée de gestionnaires performants en favorisant l’intégration, la formation continue, la rétention et une culture de travail à distance forte et inclusive.

Ce que vous apporterez:

 - Plus de 8 ans d’expérience en stratégie, FP&A (planification et analyse financière), opérations GTM ainsi qu’en gestion d’équipes.

 - Expérience dans des rôles de leadership opérationnel au sein d’un environnement de ventes SaaS mondial.

 - Expérience dans l’élaboration d’une vision stratégique, la prise de décisions d’affaires basées sur les données, l’identification des lacunes ou besoins organisationnels ainsi que le développement et le déploiement de solutions à grande échelle.

 - Expérience significative dans un rôle stratégique à fort impact au sein d’une organisation mondiale en forte croissance.

 - MBA considéré comme un atout.

 - Expérience en transformation liée à l’intelligence artificielle dans un contexte d’Opérations des revenus considérée comme un atout.

 - Solides compétences en gestion et expérience démontrée dans l’influence d’équipes au sein d’une organisation matricielle complexe.

 - Expérience éprouvée dans la direction d’équipes diversifiées et performantes réparties dans plusieurs régions géographiques.

 - Excellente capacité à interagir avec les membres de la haute direction (C-suite) et les dirigeants seniors.

source_jobs.title: Senior/Staff Product Analyst
source_jobs.description:
Hi there! Thanks for stopping by 👋



We’re looking for a Senior/Staff Product Analyst to join our Financial Services team in Canada.



This role sits at the intersection of Product Analytics, Decision Science, and Product Strategy. You will partner closely with Product, Engineering, and business leadership to help shape the future of Capital through data-driven decision making.



For Capital to succeed, many of our most important product and business decisions depend on balancing complex trade-offs across pricing, conversion, offer volume, losses, and profitability. In many ways, data-driven decision making is the product itself.



We’re looking for someone who can operate as a strategic thought partner , not simply answer predefined questions, but help determine:

 - What problems we should solve, and the right way to design and implement solutions

 - Defining and measuring success across product, merchant, and business outcomes

 - Optimizing for both merchant success and business performance, balancing growth, risk, and operational tradeoffs



What you’ll be doing:

 - Partner with Product and Engineering leaders to influence product strategy through deep analytical insights and decision frameworks

 - Lead ambiguous and high-impact analytical initiatives end-to-end, from problem definition through recommendation and implementation

 - Build analytical models and frameworks that optimize trade-offs across risk, pricing, conversion, profitability, and customer outcomes

 - Design and evaluate experiments, including pricing and conversion optimization initiatives

 - Translate complex business problems into structured analytical approaches and measurable success criteria

 - Identify product opportunities, operational inefficiencies, and strategic insights through deep analysis of large-scale transactional and product datasets

 - Deliver executive-level recommendations and influence roadmap decisions using data

 - Collaborate cross-functionally with Product, Engineering, Finance, and GTM teams to operationalize data-informed decisions



What you need to bring:

We’re looking for a senior, highly analytical candidate who is comfortable operating in ambiguity and influencing decisions at both the strategic and operational level.

You likely have experience with:

 - Advanced SQL and strong analytical skills working with large-scale product or transactional datasets

 - Python for analytics, experimentation, statistical analysis, or modeling

 - Product analytics, experimentation, and decision science methodologies

 - Building analytical frameworks that drive business and product decisions

 - Leading cross-functional analytical initiatives independently

 - Translating ambiguous business questions into structured analysis and actionable recommendations

 - Strong communication and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to influence Product and Engineering teams

 - Data visualization and storytelling experience using tools such as Looker, Tableau, or similar BI platforms

 - Experience working within product organizations at technology or fintech companies



Nice to have:

 - Experience in fintech, lending, payments, or risk-related domains

 - Experience in Data Science, predictive modeling, or optimization problems



Why this role matters:

This is not a traditional reporting or dashboard-focused analytics role.

We’re looking for someone who can act as a strategic product partner — someone who uses data to influence product direction, challenge assumptions, evaluate trade-offs, and help drive some of the most important decisions within the Capital business.

You’ll work on high-impact problems with significant visibility across Product, Engineering, and business leadership, and play a key role in shaping the future of Financial Services at Lightspeed.

source_jobs.title: Partner Marketing Specialist
source_jobs.description:
HI THERE! THANKS FOR STOPPING BY 👋

Are you actively looking for a new opportunity? Or just checking the market? Well… you might just be in the right place!

We’re looking for a Partner Marketing Specialist to join the Hospitality Partnerships team. You will be responsible for developing and executing partner-facing marketing initiatives across APAC to support partner engagement, demand generation, and product adoption.

You will work closely with Partnerships, Product, Product Marketing and Marketing teams to ensure partners are equipped with the right messaging, content and campaigns to effectively position Lightspeed, communicate joint value propositions, and drive joint outcomes.

Reporting to the Snr Manager, Partnerships - Hospitality, APAC, you will also be responsible for coordinating partner marketing activity, managing joint initiatives, and maintaining partner-facing content and assets.




WHAT YOU’LL BE DOING:

 - Collaborate cross-functionally with Partnerships, Marketing, Product, and Product Marketing teams to develop and execute integrated marketing initiatives

 - Build and manage joint marketing plans with partners that align to broader business objectives

 - Lead the coordination and execution of co-branded campaigns and content (e.g. web pages, case studies, videos, and other collateral), ensuring timely delivery and high-quality output

 - Act as a storyteller with a strong customer-centric lens, crafting clear, compelling copy across channels, including partner-facing messaging and joint value propositions

 - Develop partner-facing assets and content aligned to product roadmap and releases, working closely with Product and Product Marketing to ensure partners are equipped with current messaging and materials

 - Own and manage a pipeline of marketing projects and partner activities, including maintaining a shared calendar and communicating progress to stakeholders

 - Manage and track joint marketing initiatives and co-funded partner activity, ensuring spend is aligned to priorities and delivering measurable outcomes

 - Audit and maintain website content (including partner integrations) and third-party listings to ensure accuracy and consistency

 - Drive marketplace visibility across owned and third-party channels, working with Marketing to optimise performance where required

 - Monitor, analyse, and report on campaign performance, providing actionable recommendations for improvement

 - Support partner enablement through the creation of reusable marketing resources, tools, and training

 - Stay up to date on industry trends and the competitive landscape to identify new partnership and growth opportunities
   
   


WHAT YOU NEED TO BRING:

We're looking for a candidate who has experience with the following:

 - 2–4 years of experience across product, partner, or broader marketing roles

 - Degree in Marketing or a related field preferred

 - Strong ability to craft clear, concise, and compelling copy across multiple channels

 - Experience developing marketing collateral and content

 - Solid understanding of technology platforms and integration ecosystems

 - Experience working cross-functionally with Product, Product Marketing, Partnerships and Marketing teams

 - Experience managing multiple projects and stakeholders simultaneously, with strong organisational and prioritisation skills

 - Commercial awareness, with an understanding of how marketing activity contributes to partner engagement and growth

 - Proficiency with marketing tools and platforms, including content creation, campaign management and analytics tools

source_jobs.title: Field Sales Engineer  - Manchester
source_jobs.description:
FIELD SOLUTION CONSULTANT / SALES ENGINEER - NORTH OF ENGLAND



Location: Manchester / Field-Based (North of England)

Requirement: Full UK Driving License & willingness to travel regularly across the region.



Hi there! Thanks for stopping by 👋

Are you actively looking for a new opportunity? Or just checking the market? Well… you might just be in the right place!




ABOUT THE ROLE

We are seeking a commercially-minded, customer-facing Solution Consultant / Sales Engineer to influence and drive revenue growth across the North of England. In this role, you will be the ultimate technical partner to our Field Sales reps. You will use your expertise to position and demonstrate Lightspeed’s platform to a range of hospitality customers, helping our sales team get deals over the line.

This is a pre-sales commercial role, focused on winning new business and positioning value. You will use your deep understanding of the hospitality industry alongside your technical acumen to solve business hurdles for prospects and build ultimate customer confidence during the sales cycle.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Sales Partnership & Deal Influence: Partner closely with Account Executives to co-own the technical strategy for complex sales opportunities. Conduct high-impact, on-site discoveries and tailored product demonstrations that directly increase win rates.

 - Regional Field Mobility: Actively travel across the North of England and the Midlands to meet hospitality prospects on-site, operating with high autonomy in customer-facing environments.

 - Commercial Value Positioning: Translate complex technical features (software, hardware, APIs) into tangible business value and ROI for restaurateurs and hospitality operators.

 - Industry & Competitive Expertise: Maintain an acute understanding of the hospitality tech landscape. Act as a trusted advisor to prospects, helping sales teams effectively differentiate Lightspeed from regional competitors.

 - Technical Risk Assessment: Identify potential technical blockers or integration constraints early in the sales cycle to ensure we are setting up both the customer and our implementation teams for long-term success.

 - Trade Shows and Industry Events: Represent the company as the technical and product expert at major hospitality trade shows, engaging prospects and supporting the field team with live environments.

 - Cross-Functional Alignment: Act as the bridge between the field and internal teams, ensuring clear documentation of customer requirements for a smooth handoff to Implementation once the deal is won.
   
   


WHAT YOU NEED TO BRING (REQUIREMENTS)

Sales & Hospitality Experience:

 - Commercial Sales Background: Demonstrated experience working directly within a sales process (e.g., as a Sales Engineer, Pre-Sales Consultant, or Tech-Savvy Account Executive). You understand sales methodologies and how to help move a prospect from interest to decision.

 - Hospitality DNA: A strong, deep understanding of the hospitality industry (restaurants, hotels, bars) and the unique operational challenges operators face. Direct experience working in hospitality, or selling technology into hospitality, is highly required.

 - Commercial Focus: A mindset centered on value creation, persuasion and solution-design.

Travel & Logistics:

 - Mobility: A valid UK driving license and access to a vehicle are essential.

 - Flexibility: Genuine willingness to travel regularly across the North of England and Midlands for on-site customer deep-dives.

Technical Competencies:

 - Solution Mindset: Strong aptitude for cloud-based SaaS, APIs, and integrations, with the ability to explain complex technical workflows simply to independent restaurant owners or C-level hospitality executives.

 - Communication Excellence: Exceptional relationship-building skills, with the ability to command a room and collaborate seamlessly with internal sales reps.

source_jobs.description:
Hi there! Thanks for stopping by 👋

Are you actively looking for a new opportunity? Or just checking the market? Well… you might just be in the right place!

Lightspeed is looking for a highly motivated Outbound Sales Development Representative to join our North American Outbound Sales team. You’ll help drive revenue growth by generating and qualifying leads for SMB and mid-market retail clients, acting as the first point of contact and helping empower retailers.

Our Outbound team is in hyper-growth mode, and we need a driven, strategic, and energetic individual who is passionate about helping larger retail businesses grow. If you’re results-driven and excited to join a fast-growing company, this is the role for you.

What you’ll be doing:

 - Proactively prospecting SMB and mid-market retail accounts via phone, email, social media, and other outbound channels.

 - Reaching out to different stakeholders to understand their challenges and build strong relationships.

 - Collaborating with Account Executives to ensure smooth lead handoff and strategic follow-up.

 - Providing feedback to marketing and sales teams based on prospect conversations to help refine outbound strategies.

 - Consistently achieving monthly, quarterly, and annual outbound sales goals in a high-velocity environment.

What you’ll be bringing:

 - 1+ years of sales or customer facing experience

 - Strong understanding of the sales cycle

 - Strong communication and relationship-building skills, with the ability to explain complex solutions to multiple stakeholders.

 - Tech-savvy, strategic, and comfortable navigating sales accounts.

 - Entrepreneurial mindset, bias toward action, and the ability to adapt quickly in a fast-growing business.

 - As a global company with employees and clients outside of Quebec, fluency in English as a working language is required for this position.

Even better if you have:

 - 1-3 years of experience in SaaS outbound sales, preferably with SMB or mid-market retail clients.

 - Ability to cold-call, email, and engage SMB and mid-market stakeholders in complex sales cycles.

 - Familiarity with POS systems or other software platforms in large organizations.

source_jobs.title: Chargé(e) de Comptes - Détaillants
source_jobs.description:
Bonjour! Merci de ta visite 👋

Es-tu activement à la recherche d’une nouvelle opportunité? Ou tu ne fais que jeter un coup d’œil au marché? Eh bien… tu te trouves au bon endroit!

Nous sommes à la recherche d’un(e) Chargé(e) de comptes qui se joindra à notre équipe notre équipe de vente aux détaillants. Sous la responsabilité du directeur du développement des affaires, vous serez chargé de générer des revenus par le biais de la correspondance téléphonique et électronique, ainsi que des présentations par partage d'écran aux clients potentiels. Votre objectif final est de créer des solutions pour les détaillants qui cherchent à adopter Lightspeed pour leur activité. Nous recherchons un professionnel de la vente énergique, ayant l'expérience de la vente de solutions technologiques, et qui ne craindra pas un environnement rapide et centré sur le client. En tant que personne ambitieuse et prospère, vous aurez accès à un potentiel de gains importants avec une commission non plafonnée !

Ce dont vous serez responsable :

 - Assurer le suivi et la gestion des prospects qualifiés par téléphone et par courrier électronique dans les délais impartis

 - Travailler dans un environnement d'équipe pour faire croître l'entreprise en collaboration

 - Diriger des présentations et des démonstrations individuelles de partage d'écran selon les besoins

 - Travailler sur les négociations de prix

 - Atteindre et dépasser les quotas de vente mensuels, trimestriels et annuels

 - Travailler avec les représentants du développement des ventes afin de coordonner les rendez-vous et d'optimiser le calendrier

 - Diriger efficacement les prospects dans le cadre d'un processus de vente défini

 - Suivre la progression des prospects jusqu'à la clôture dans Salesforce.com http://Salesforce.com

 - Assumer d'autres tâches et responsabilités définies par la direction

Ce que tu apporteras à l'équipe :

 - Expérience dans la vente, de préférence dans un environnement lié à la vente de solutions ou à la technologie

 - De solides compétences en matière de présentation et de contact téléphonique

 - Expérience et solide compréhension des devis, de la facturation et des achats

 - Excellente maîtrise de l'anglais et du français, à l'écrit et à l'oral

 - Passion pour les gens et la technologie

 - Excellentes compétences en matière de service à la clientèle

 - Capacité à travailler sous pression et dans un environnement ciblé

 - Horaire flexible

Critères appréciés, sans être indispensables :

 - Expérience avec Salesforce.com http://Salesforce.com

 - Expérience avec SaaS

 - Expérience de la vente au détaillants

 - Compréhension des principes comptables

 - Expérience des bases de données, des points de vente ou des systèmes ERP

Nous savons qu’une personne ne se résume pas à son CV. Si tu ne sais pas si ton profil convient au poste, clique sur le bouton « Postuler » pour le découvrir!

Ce que tu as à gagner :

Viens vivre l’expérience Lightspeed…

 - Tu auras l’occasion de travailler dans un milieu réellement flexible et hybride

 - Tu profiteras de possibilités de carrière authentiques au sein d’une entreprise qui crée constamment de nouveaux emplois

 - Tu travailleras au sein d’une équipe assez grande pour la croissance, mais suffisamment petite pour avoir un réel impact

… et profiter d’une gamme d’avantages qui te procureront bonheur, santé et prospérité :

 - Programme d’actionnariat Lightspeed (nous sommes tous propriétaires)

 - Politique sur les congés payés illimités

Bonjour! Merci de ta visite 👋

Es-tu activement à la recherche d’une nouvelle opportunité? Ou tu ne fais que jeter un coup d’œil au marché? Eh bien… tu te trouves au bon endroit!

Nous sommes à la recherche d’un(e) représentant(e) au développement des ventes pour rejoindre notre équipe (détaillants). Sous la responsabilité de la gestionnaire des nouveaux marchés, tu seras le premier point de contact de nos clients potentiels avec notre équipe de vente.  - Vérifier les besoins des clients afin de déterminer si les solutions Lightspeed leur conviennent

Key Account Manager -  (deutschsprachig, Berlin, m/f/x)
source_jobs.description:
Lightspeed sucht einen erfahrenen, strategisch denkenden Key Account Manager (m/w/x), der/die unsere wichtigsten Enterprise-Kunden im Gastronomie-Segment langfristig entwickelt, begeistert und eng begleitet.

In dieser Rolle agierst du als zentrale Ansprechperson für unsere größten Hospitality-Kunden in der DACH-Region. Du bist verantwortlich für das Wachstum, die Produktnutzung und das Stakeholder-Management innerhalb deines Portfolios – mit dem Ziel, echte Business-Partnerschaften aufzubauen. Dabei nutzt du Daten, Feedback und deine SaaS-Expertise, um Mehrwert auf Enterprise-Level zu schaffen. 

 



*** Bitte beachte, dass diese Rolle in Berlin, Deutschland besetzt wird & einem hybriden Arbeitsmodell folgt.***

 

 

Was du bei uns machst:

 - Du agierst als Hauptansprechpartner*in für unsere größten und wichtigsten Kunden in der Gastronomie

 - Du entwickelst und steuerst strategische Account-Pläne zur Förderung von Wachstum, Retention und Produktausschöpfung

 - Du arbeitest proaktiv an der Produktakzeptanz und stellst sicher, dass der Net Revenue Retention (NRR) in deinem Portfolio steigt

 - Du planst und führst vierteljährliche Business Reviews und Erfolgspläne durch

 - Du analysierst Nutzungsdaten, Go-Live-Milestones und Health Scores, um Risiken frühzeitig zu erkennen und zu adressieren

 - Du baust starke, vertrauensvolle Beziehungen zu C-Level- und operativen Stakeholdern auf – mit dem Ziel, Lightspeed als langfristigen strategischen Partner zu etablieren

 - Du arbeitest funktionsübergreifend mit Product, Customer Success, Implementation und Support, um eine exzellente Customer Experience sicherzustellen
   
   

 

 

Was du mitbringst:

 - 3–5+ Jahre Erfahrung im Account Management oder Strategischem Customer Success im B2B SaaS-Umfeld, idealerweise mit Enterprise-Kunden

 - Du bringst ein tiefes Verständnis für SaaS-Metriken (NRR, Product Adoption, Expansion, Renewals, Churn) mit und nutzt sie zur Portfoliosteuerung

 - Du hast ein starkes Gefühl von ownership, bist hands-on, datengetrieben und gleichzeitig stark in der Beziehungspflege

 - Du hast Erfahrung im Umgang mit mehrstufigen Stakeholder-Strukturen und bewegst dich souverän zwischen C-Level, Ops und Tech

 - Du sprichst verhandlungssicher Deutsch und Englisch

 - Kenntnisse in der Gastronomie-, POS- oder Hospitality-Tech-Branche sind ein Plus, aber kein Muss

 

 

 

Was du von Lightspeed erwarten kannst:

 - Work from Anywhere: Arbeite bis zu 60 Tage im Jahr remote – weltweit

 - Modernes Arbeiten: hybrides Modelle, was Flexibilität bietet

 - Aktienbeteiligung: Nimm am Lightspeed Stock Option Program teil

 - 400 € Health & Wellness Budget: Für deine physische und mentale Gesundheit

 - Mental Health Support via Modern Health App

 - Impact-orientiertes Umfeld: Kleines, agiles Team mit globaler Vision

source_jobs.title: Account Executive  (Berlin, m/f/x)
source_jobs.description:
Ready to drive real growth in a hyper-growth SaaS?

Lightspeed ist ein weltweit führendes SaaS-Unternehmen, das den Handel neu definiert. Unsere cloudbasierte Commerce-Plattform hilft Retail- und Hospitality-Unternehmen dabei, ihre Umsätze zu skalieren, Kundenerlebnisse zu verbessern und grenzenlos zu wachsen.

Und auch wir wachsen – und zwar schnell!

Deshalb suchen wir smarte, ambitionierte Sales-Talente, die Lust auf echte Verantwortung haben – und darauf, gemeinsam mit uns neue Kund*innen zu begeistern.

Als Account Executive übernimmst du die Verantwortung für ein wachstumsstarkes Kundenportfolio in der Hospitality-Branche und treibst gemeinsam mit deinen Kund*innen die Skalierung ihres Geschäfts voran – mit den innovativen Financial-Services-Lösungen von LightspeedDu bist eine Macherin, die*der nicht nur verkauft, sondern echten Mehrwert schafft? Du liebst es, in einem schnelllebigen Umfeld zu performen und mit smarten SaaS- & FinTech-Lösungen echten Impact zu erzielen? Dann bist du bei uns genau richtig.

 



 

Deine Mission als Account Executive:



Du bist nicht nur Teil unseres Sales-Teams – du bist die treibende Kraft, wenn es darum geht, neue Kund*innen zu gewinnen und echtes Wachstum zu pushen. 



 - Du identifizierst echte Chancen: In Discovery-Calls findest du präzise heraus, was unsere potenziellen Kund*innen brauchen – und wie Lightspeed ihnen den entscheidenden Vorsprung verschaffen kann
   
   

 - Du pitchst wie ein Profi: Mit überzeugenden, zielgerichteten Demos zeigst du den Mehrwert unserer Plattform und führst deine Deals von der ersten Kontaktaufnahme bis zum erfolgreichen Closing
   
   

 - Du hältst mehrere Bälle gleichzeitig in der Luft: Komplexe Sales-Zyklen managen? Parallele Prozesse orchestrieren? Für dich Standard
   
   

 - Du arbeitest eng mit SDRs & Marketing zusammen, um eine starke, prall gefüllte Pipeline aufzubauen – und kontinuierlich zu befeuern
   
   

 - Du denkst über den Deal hinaus: Du bringst dich in strategische Initiativen ein, denkst in Kundenerlebnissen und treibst so unser MRR-Wachstum aktiv voran
   
   
   

Was du mitbringst:



 - Erfahrung im B2B-Sales – ob als SDR oder bereits in einer Full-Cycle-Rolle: du weißt, wie man Deals closed
   
   

 - Idealerweise kommst du aus dem SaaS-, IT-, E-Commerce-, POS- oder Hospitality-Umfeld
   
   

 - Outbound-Mentalität: Du wartest nicht, du gehst raus und holst dir deine Deals
   
   

 - Du liebst den Kundenkontakt, kommunizierst klar und hast ein echtes Gespür für Sales-Momente
   
   

 - Du sprichst fließend Deutsch und bringst gute Englischkenntnisse mit
   
   

Nicht sicher, ob du zu 100 % passt? Wenn du Sales im Blut hast, solltest du dich bewerben – wir wollen dich kennenlernen.

 

Was dich bei Lightspeed erwartet:



 - Ein starkes Growth-Programm für deine Weiterentwicklung und Karriereziele
   
   

 - Echte Aufstiegschancen in einem Unternehmen, das täglich neue Jobs schafft
   
   

 - Ein Team, das groß genug ist für echte Perspektiven, aber lean genug, um deinen Impact zu spür

 - Hybrides Arbeitsmodell 

Und natürlich viele weitere Benefits, die dich happy und gesund halten:



 - Lightspeed-Aktienprogramm – wir sind alle Mitinhaber*innen
   
   

 - 400 EUR Health & Wellness Budget jährlich für dein Wohlbefinden
   
   

 - Mental Health Support via Modern Health App
   
   

 - "Work from Anywhere" Option: arbeite bis zu 60 Tage im Jahr weltweit, wo du möchtest
   
   

 - Freier Zugang zu LinkedIn Learning und deinem persönlichen Karriere-GPS
   
   

 - Freistellung für ehrenamtliche Arbeit
   
   

 - Unterstützung für öffentliche Verkehrsmittel
   
   

 - Regelmäßige Team-Lunches, Frühstücks und Snacks im Berliner Büro

 

Ready to close your next big deal – mit uns? Dann klick auf „Apply“ und let’s scale! 🚀

source_jobs.title: Senior Director, Growth Marketing
source_jobs.description:
Hi there, thanks for stopping by!

Are you actively looking for a new opportunity? Or just checking the market? Well… you might just be in the right place!



At Lightspeed, we’re looking for Senior Director, Growth Marketing to join our global team and lead the charge in creating data-driven growth strategies that drive revenue, optimize performance, and push our global customer acquisition efforts into new territory. You’ll be a key leader and owner in Lightspeed’s global marketing organization. Your mission will be to build scalable growth engines, improve conversion funnels, and enable data-first decision-making across our acquisition and lifecycle efforts.

 

What you’ll be doing:

 - Owning the strategy, performance, and leadership of four core teams: 
   
   - MAT (Marketing Automation), 
   
   - CRO (Conversion Rate Optimization), 
   
   - Marketing Analytics,
   
   - Marketing AI.

 - Lead and Develop Teams: Oversee and grow a high-performing global team across MAT, CRO,AI and Data, fostering collaboration, accountability, and innovation.

 - Growth Strategy: Define and execute global growth strategies that align with Lightspeed’s business goals across markets and verticals.

 - Data-Driven Optimization: Drive the use of experimentation and data analytics to optimize lead generation, lead scoring, conversion funnels, and retention efforts.

 - Marketing Automation: Own the global marketing tech stack and automation strategy to ensure scalable and efficient execution of campaigns.

 - Conversion Rate Optimization: Lead CRO efforts across web properties, collaborating with UX, design, and content teams to continually test and improve user journeys.

 - Marketing AI: working closely with the Information Systems (IS) team to implement new ways to leverage AI for marketing effectiveness and efficiency, including collaborating with our different sales teams to enrich our prospects and customers profile using genAI.

 - Performance Tracking: Partner with our Data Office to define KPIs, build dashboards, and create a culture of performance measurement and iterative improvement.

 - Stakeholder Collaboration: Work cross-functionally with regional leaders, product teams, and revenue operations to align on growth priorities and execution plans.

 - Innovation & Experimentation: Establish a strong experimentation framework and test-and-learn culture within all teams under your purview.
   
   

What you need to bring:

 - 8–12+ years of experience in growth marketing, performance marketing, or marketing operations, ideally in a global SaaS or eCommerce environment.

 - 5+ years of experience leading teams, including cross-functional and remote teams.

 - Proven success in managing or scaling Marketing Automation/AI, CRO, and/or Data teams.

 - Deep understanding of modern marketing tech stacks (e.g., Salesforce, HubSpot/Marketo, Google Cloud Platform, BigQuery, Fivetran, Looker, GA4, Clay (CMS, CDPs).

 - Strong analytical mindset with a track record of leveraging data to drive marketing performance.

 - Experience in high-growth, fast-paced environments and a passion for experimentation and continuous improvement.

 - Excellent communication and leadership skills, with a collaborative, global mindset.

 - As a global company with employees and clients outside of Quebec, fluency in English as a working language is required for this position.

 

Even better if you have, but not necessary:

 - Experience in the POS, retail tech, or hospitality tech industries.

 - Familiarity with privacy regulations (GDPR, CCPA) as it relates to data collection and marketing practices.


You’ll enjoy:

 - A flexible work environment that empowers you to do your best work

 - A culture that celebrates performance

 - The chance to make an impact in a team that’s big enough for career growth, but lean enough to make your voice heard

 - Career-defining opportunities
   
   

Plus benefits designed to keep you happy, healthy and fulfilled.

 - F

source_jobs.title: Sr Directeur/Directrice, Marketing de Croissance
source_jobs.description:
Bonjour! Merci de ta visite 👋

Es-tu activement à la recherche d’une nouvelle opportunité? Ou tu ne fais que jeter un coup d’œil au marché? Eh bien… tu te trouves au bon endroit!

Chez Lightspeed, on cherche un ou une Directeur/Directrice, Marketing de Croissance pour joindre notre équipe mondiale. Ton rôle sera crucial: piloter la création de stratégies data-driven qui génèrent du revenu, optimisent la performance et propulsent notre acquisition client à l’échelle internationale. Tu seras un.e leader incontournable et tu seras responsable de l’organisation marketing de manière globale chez Lightspeed. Ta mission principale? Bâtir des moteurs de croissance évolutifs, peaufiner nos entonnoirs de conversation et ancrer la prise de décision sur les données pour tout ce qui concerne l’acquisition et le cycle de vie.

Les responsabilités :

 - Piloter la stratégie, la performance et le leadership de quatre équipes fondamentales :
   
   - MAT (Automatisation du Marketing),
   
   - CRO (Optimisation du Taux de Conversion),
   
   - Analyse Marketing,
   
   - IA Marketing.

 - Diriger et développer les équipes : superviser et faire grandir une équipe mondiale hautement performante (Automatisation, CRO, IA et Data), tout en favorisant la collaboration, la responsabilisation et l’innovation. 

 - Stratégie de croissance : définir et exécuter des stratégies de croissance globales et alignées sur les objectifs d’affaires de Lightspeed, et ce, à travers tous nos marchés et verticales.

 - Optimisation axée sur les données : encourager l'expérimentation et l'analyse de données afin d’optimiser la génération et l’évaluation de leads, les entonnoirs de conversion et les efforts de rétention.

 - Automatisation du marketing : gérer l'ensemble de notre tech stack marketing et la stratégie d'automatisation pour garantir une exécution évolutive et efficace des campagnes.

 - Optimisation du taux de conversion : mener les efforts de CRO sur nos propriétés web, en collaborant avec les équipes UX, design et contenu pour éprouver et améliorer continuellement les parcours utilisateur.

 - IA marketing : collaborer avec l'équipe des Systèmes d'Information (SI) pour implanter de nouvelles façons d’exploiter l'IA au service de l'efficacité marketing, incluant la collaboration avec nos différentes équipes de vente pour enrichir les profils prospects/clients grâce à l'IA générative (genAI).

 - Suivi de la performance : collaborer avec l’équipe Data pour définir les ICP (indicateurs clés de performance), créer des tableaux de bord et instaurer une culture de mesure et d’amélioration itérative de la performance.

 - Collaboration avec les parties prenantes : travailler de manière transversale avec les leaders régionaux, les équipes produits et les opérations de revenus pour aligner les priorités de croissance et les plans d'exécution.

 - Innovation et expérimentation : établir un cadre d'expérimentation solide et une culture de test-and-learn au sein de toutes les équipes.

Ta valeur ajoutée :

 - 8 à 12+ années d'expérience en marketing de croissance, marketing de performance ou opérations marketing, idéalement dans un environnement SaaS ou e-commerce mondial.

 - 5+ années d'expérience dans la gestion d'équipes, incluant des équipes transversales et à distance.

 - Capacités démontrées à gérer et à faire croître des équipes d'Automatisation/IA Marketing, CRO et/ou Data.

 - Compréhension approfondie des tech stacks marketing modernes (par exemple, Salesforce, HubSpot/Marketo, Google Cloud Platform, BigQuery, Fivetran, Looker, GA4, Clay (CMS, CDPs).

 - Solide esprit d'analyse et expérience démontrée à exploiter des données pour améliorer la performance marketing.

 - Expérience dans des environnements à forte croissance, ainsi qu’une passion pour l'expérimentation et l'amélioration continue.

 - Excellentes compétences en communication et en leadership, avec un état d'esprit collaboratif et global.

 - Anglais fonctionnel requis: en tant qu'entreprise

source_jobs.title: Support Specialist, German speaking
source_jobs.description:
Hi there! Thanks for stopping by 👋

Are you actively looking for a new opportunity? Or just checking the market? Well… you might just be in the right place!

As part of Lightspeed’s Support team, you will be the first point of contact for our customers who need assistance, helping with a wide variety of requests all the way from general education to hardware/software investigation and everything in between. 

Working closely with various global teams at Lightspeed, you will investigate, troubleshoot, test and resolve issues that our hospitality customers may be facing during service. 




WHAT YOU’LL BE DOING:

 - Working with a truly global, multi-language 24x7 team across APAC, EMEA and NOAM

 - Flexibility on working days and shifts (weekends included)

 - Have enthusiastic, positive and knowledgeable interactions with our Hospitality customers, partners and Lightspeed team members, maintaining Lightspeed's Gold Class standard for support via phone, chat and email.

 - Use consistent troubleshooting techniques to quickly and efficiently identify the source of customer issues and provide a prompt and appropriate solution

 - Work within our customer support platforms to document, track and resolve interactions

 - Assist with the identification, reproduction and escalation of product issues and bugs to the Quality Assurance team, tracking through to resolution with each customer, partner and Lightspeed team member

 - Advocate and promote improvements to our products and services

 - Operating within a roster/schedule to ensure high availability of our assistance for our customers (In collaboration with your manager)

 

What you’ll be bringing to the team:

 - Previous customer service experience and/or technical support Strong attention to detail with an emphasis on providing an exceptional customer experience

 - Excellent verbal and written communication skills in German & English

 - Basic experience using common online, mobile and desktop platforms; iOS, Android, OSX, Windows

Even better if you have, but not necessary:

 - Experience interacting with customers through Phone, Email, Ticketing and Live Chat platforms

 - Experience or knowledge of the Hospitality industry

 - Experience supporting a SaaS product

 - Experience supporting an iOS application

 - Computer networking experience or interest

 

Even better if you have, but not necessary

 - Passion in technology;

 - Working knowledge of Mac OS and basic knowledge of HTML/CSS;

 - Hospitality experience;

 - Dutch or French written and verbal communication skills;

We know that people are more than what’s on their CV. If you’re unsure that you have the right profile for the role... hit the ‘Apply’ button and give it a try!
Please make sure to apply with a CV in English.


What’s in it for you:

 - Unlimited Annual Leave with our PTO scheme

 - Opportunity to join a growing team, in a fast-paced, high-growth company

 - Work with a team of incredibly talented and forward-thinking people!

In short, an opportunity to learn, expand your skill set, forge wonderful relationships and make your mark within the diverse and inclusive Lightspeed family, a true Canadian tech success story.

source_jobs.description:
CX [EXPANSION] ENABLEMENT MANAGER

Location: Berlin, Ghent 

This role requires being a native speaker of the region to ensure full linguistic and cultural fluency.


PURPOSE OF THE ROLE

Part of the Revenue [GTM] Enablement Team, reporting to Manager, Revenue Enablement, in this role you will be leading the design, development, implementation and measurement of training initiatives for both new hires and current team members in the Customers Organization with a key focus on the the Expansion Team, consisting of 50+ Account Managers, ensuring they are equipped with the skills and certifications needed to deliver outstanding customer experiences. This role offers a unique opportunity to impact our customers by enhancing the capabilities of our internal front-line teams.

source_jobs.title: Principal Product Designer, Retail
source_jobs.description:
Hi there! Thanks for stopping by 👋

Are you actively looking for a new opportunity? Or just checking the market? Well… you might just be in the right place!

We're looking for a Principal Product Designer — a rare, senior individual-contributor role reporting directly to the Head of Design for Retail. Lightspeed AI is our biggest bet: an intelligence layer that acts like a partner for every merchant, evolving from an assistant that answers questions into an agent that takes action. Almost nothing about designing this experience has an established playbook — how merchants delegate work to an agent, supervise it, learn to trust it, and stay in control. You'll help write that playbook.

The role has two centers of gravity. First, leading design for our agentic AI experience — the defining product challenge of the next several years. Second, raising the craft bar across the entire Retail design organization: not by writing standards, but by making things — designing alongside teams in every product area to show, concretely, what great looks like. You'll also help shape our mobile point-of-sale strategy as a secondary focus.

This is not a management role. It's for a maker who moves fluidly between defining strategy and pushing pixels — someone who can hold the full context of a large product portfolio and still be the strongest hands-on designer in the room.

Location: Toronto preferred; open to other locations in Ontario, Quebec and Eastern Canada.

What you'll be doing:

 - Define new interaction patterns for agentic AI — designing how merchants converse with, direct, supervise, and build trust in an intelligent agent that works on their behalf.

 - Lead design for our AI experience end-to-end as a hands-on IC: from concept and prototype through polished, shipped product, in close partnership with AI engineering and product leadership.

 - Raise the craft bar across the organization through direct contribution — jumping into any product area to explore, elevate, and unblock the most important work, and leaving teams stronger than you found them.

 - Lead vision work that rethinks core merchant experiences through hands-on exploration and prototyping — concept work ambitious enough to shape strategy and concrete enough to build.

 - Prototype with AI, daily: work AI-first — using modern tools to move from intent to working prototype fast — and model this way of working for designers across the org.

 - Shape our mobile strategy with design thinking and hands-on support, as an ongoing secondary focus.

 - Influence without authority: align designers, leaders, and cross-functional partners across multiple teams through clarity, storytelling, and the quality of the work itself.

What you need to bring:

 - 12+ years of product design experience with a maker's mindset — moving fluidly between strategic thinking and detailed, polished execution.

 - A portfolio demonstrating exceptional hands-on craft across UX, interaction, and visual design — work that other designers point to.

 - Experience leading large, ambiguous initiatives across multiple teams and delivering measurable outcomes — without formal authority.

 - Genuine fluency with AI: active use of LLMs and AI prototyping tools in your workflow, and a strong point of view on designing AI-powered products.

 - Enough technical understanding to be a real partner to engineers — you can build prototypes to communicate ideas and reason about constraints.

 - The ability to influence senior stakeholders through clear communication, storytelling, and tangible design work.

 - Taste. You know what great looks and feels like, and you push — and help others push — to get there.

Your mission is to put Lightspeed Hospitality on the map across Germany and the UK. You won’t just be managing channels: you’ll launch our Instagram presence in Germany and expand our voice on LinkedIn and YouTube across Germany and the UK.

Working closely with Content, Field, Product, PR and Regional Marketing teams, you’ll turn priorities into compelling social storytelling. As both a “story scout” and brand gatekeeper, you’ll uncover great hospitality stories while ensuring everything we publish is premium and impactful.

You’ll be responsible for:

 - Regional storytelling: Proactively connect with Hospitality clients to uncover unique stories. Lead creative ideation and bring forward concepts that deliver authentic, scroll-stopping content.

 - Workflow management: Use Monday.com http://Monday.com to track timelines and Jira to manage creative deliverables, ensuring projects are delivered on time.

 - Strategic Thinker: You always see the bigger picture. You know how to align your social media efforts with broader marketing goals to drive real business impact.

source_jobs.title: Local Growth & Field Community Lead
source_jobs.description:
We want to further connect hyper-locally with our hospitality customers and prospects in key cities in Germany, and ensure Lightspeed is top of mind when they think of their tech partner. That’s where you come in: you’ll be the ultimate connector, part event planner, part content creator, and part relationship manager, owning the local vibe and growth strategy from the ground up!

What you'll do:

 - Launch the Anchor Mission: Map out and enlist our most respected existing customers: your local legends! You’ll use them as the "anchors" to host killer events, attracting new prospects and key local industry influencers (like top chefs, consultants, and power players).

 - Network Ninja: Identify and forge strong relationships with all the right external stakeholders: key prospects, influencers, and local entities (think trade associations) to build an unbeatable local network.

 - Throw "Scrappy but Cool" Events: Ideate, run and measure the impact of high-vibe local gatherings, from quick morning breakfasts and working lunches to exclusive late-night networking, hosted right at customer sites to build a real community.

 - Boots-on-the-Ground Content Producer: Forget the studio. Grab your phone and capture "raw and real" customer stories working in close collaboration with our social team. You’ll be the one capturing the unique moment that will be turned into viral video snippets for social media and compelling case studies for our sales decks.

 - Fuel the Sales Fire: Keep local Field sales materials absolutely fresh and armed with the latest data and compelling stories you gather from your cities.

 - Internal Collaborator: You won’t fly solo! You'll partner closely with Regional Integrated Marketing Managers (for overall strategy), Regional Field Sales teams (turn your work into revenue), Account Managers (expand your footprint among existing customers), Product Marketing (increase awareness of our products), Social Media Specialists and Content (for creative and tool inputs) and Partnership Marketing (for fun local brand collabs).

How you'll track your success:

This is where the magic becomes measurable. You will be held accountable for making your offline work translate into online growth.

 - The Link-Up: You'll ensure clear pipeline attribution, being able to directly link every local event or influencer campaign you run to sales metrics. 

 - Setting the Vibe: Partner with IMMs to set targets for both awareness (how loud is the vibe online?), attendance, number of events to deliver, and with Local Field Managers to set consideration targets (how many leads and demos are we driving?) for your specific cities.

 - Data Storyteller: Create simple, powerful, data-backed monthly reports that tell the story of a marketing activity leading directly to brand engagement and sales wins.

 - Future-Proof Analytics: Use tools to quickly analyse event feedback, gauge sentiment from social comments, or pull insights from CRM data and Looker to see which city-specific tactics are actually moving the needle.

What it takes to succeed:

 - Hospitality DNA: You're a natural in the hospitality world, with experience and contacts to prove it.

 - Scrappy Creator: You're a one-person content studio! You can film, edit, and write good-quality content solo using basic tools, Canva, Apple Suite (iMovie) or Google Suite, and you use AI to supercharge your workflow.

 - Your Office is the Streets: You love being out of the office and "in the streets" of your assigned cities to make sure you connect with our audience. 

 - Strategic & Independent: You’re a self-starter. You don’t wait for a script; you see a golden opportunity, and you run with it.

 - Sales-Minded Hustler: You instinctively understand the sales cycle and know exactly how to track the impact of your amazing offline work on tangible online growth.

 - Event Diva: You know how to run the show and bring a strong experience in events, ideally in hospitality. 

Nice to have: 

 - CRM

notes: Lightspeed is a large, established company with a strong focus on sales and revenue growth. While they do have some technical roles, the majority of their job postings are for sales, marketing, and customer success positions.
